flowcharts.title: Management of Stage I (T1a) Renal Cell Carcinoma

nodes.label: Clinical stage T1a (tumor ≤4 cm, confined to kidney)

edges.label: Preferred. Category 2A. Recommended for patients with solitary kidney, bilateral tumors, chronic kidney disease, or a normal contralateral kidney. [KID-1]
Useful in certain circumstances. Category 2A for T1a. Suitable for patients who are not surgical candidates or prefer a less invasive approach. [KID-1]
Useful in certain circumstances. Category 2A. For non-optimal surgical candidates. Requires referral to high-volume center. [KID-1, KID-B]

Useful in certain circumstances. Category 2A. For patients with small renal masses, competing risks of death, or significant comorbidities. Entails serial imaging with timely intervention if growth is noted. [KID-1]
Other recommended. Category 2A. In select patients if partial nephrectomy is not technically feasible. [KID-1]

flowcharts.title: Management of Stage I (T1b) Renal Cell Carcinoma

nodes.label: Clinical stage T1b (tumor >4 cm but ≤7 cm, confined to kidney)

edges.label: Preferred. Category 2A. Recommended if technically feasible and provides oncologic outcomes comparable to radical nephrectomy. [KID-1]
Preferred. Category 2A. An alternative if partial nephrectomy is not feasible. [KID-1]
Useful in certain circumstances. Category 2A. In select patients with competing risks. [KID-1]
Useful in certain circumstances. Category 2B. In select patients not eligible for surgery. [KID-1]
Useful in certain circumstances. Category 2A. For non-optimal surgical candidates. [KID-1, KID-B]

flowcharts.title: Management of Stage II Renal Cell Carcinoma

edges.label: Preferred. Category 2A. Radical nephrectomy is standard; partial nephrectomy may be considered if technically feasible. [KID-2]
Useful in certain circumstances. Category 2B. For non-optimal surgical candidates. [KID-2, KID-B]
Preferred (for Grade 4 tumors). Category 1. For Grade 4 tumors with clear cell histology ± sarcomatoid features. [KID-2]
Preferred (for Grade 4 tumors). Category 2A. For Grade 4 tumors with clear cell histology ± sarcomatoid features. [KID-2]
Useful in certain circumstances. Category 2A. An option if adjuvant therapy is not indicated or chosen. [KID-2]
